author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the Lord’s Prayer in St. Matthew’s Gospel, Chap. vi. 9, etc. to the Competentes.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the Lord’s Prayer in St. Matthew’s Gospel, Chap. vi. 9, etc. to the Competentes.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The blessed Apostle, to show that those times when it should come to pass that all the nations should believe in Christ had been foretold by the Prophets, produced this testimony where it is written, “And it shall be, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord, shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For before time the name of the Lord who made heaven and earth was called upon amongst the Israelites only; the rest of the nations called upon dumb and deaf idols, by whom they were not heard, or by devils, by whom they were heard to their harm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But when the fulness of time came,” that was fulfilled which had been foretold, “And it shall be, that whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord, shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Moreover, because the Jews, even those who believed in Christ, grudged the Gospel to the Gentiles, and said that the Gospel ought not to be preached to them who were not circumcised; because against these the Apostle Paul alleged this testimony, “And it shall be, that whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord, shall be saved;” he immediately subjoined, to convince those who were unwilling that the Gospel should be preached to the Gentiles, the words, “But how shall they call upon Him, in whom they have not believed? or how shall they believe in Him of whom they have not heard? or how shall they hear without a preacher? or how shall they preach except they be sent?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then he said, “how shall they call upon Him in whom they have not believed?” ye have not first learnt the Lord’s Prayer, and after that the Creed; but first the Creed, where ye might know what to believe, and afterwards the Prayer, where ye might know whom to call upon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Creed then has respect to the faith, the Lord’s Prayer to prayer; because it is he who believeth, that is heard when he calleth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But many ask for what they ought not to ask, not knowing what is expedient for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Two things therefore must he that prays beware of; that he ask not what he ought not; and that he ask not from whom he ought not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From the devil, from idols, from evil spirits, must nothing be asked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From the Lord our God Jesus Christ, God the Father of Prophets, and Apostles, and Martyrs, from the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, from God who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all things in them, from Him must we ask whatsoever we have to ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we must beware that we ask not of Him that which we ought not to ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If because we ought to ask for life, thou ask it of dumb and deaf idols, what doth it profit thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So if from God the Father, who is in heaven, thou dost wish for the death of thine enemies, what doth it profit thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard or read in the Psalm, in which the damnable end of the traitor Judas is foretold, how the prophecy spake of him, “Let his prayer be turned into sin?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then thou risest up, and prayest for evil on thine enemies, thy “prayer will be turned into sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You have read in the Holy Psalms, how that he who speaks in them imprecates, as it would seem, many curses upon his enemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And surely, one may say, he who speaks in the Psalms is a righteous man; wherefore then does he so wish evil upon his enemies?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He does not wish, but he foresees, it is a prophecy of one who is telling things to come, not a vow of malediction; for the prophets knew by the Spirit to whom evil was appointed to happen, and to whom good; and by prophecy they spake as if they wished for what they did foresee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how canst thou know whether he for whom today thou art asking evil, may not to-morrow be a better man than thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But you will say, I know him to be a wicked man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well: thou must know that thou art wicked too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Although it may be thou takest upon thyself to judge of another’s heart what thou dost not know; but as for thine own self thou knowest that thou art wicked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hearest thou not the Apostle saying, “Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when the Apostle Paul persecuted the Christians, binding them wherever he found them, and drew them to the Chief Priests to be questioned and punished, what think ye, brethren, did the Church pray against him, or for him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Surely the Church of God which had learnt instruction from her Lord, who said as He hung upon the Cross, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do,” so prayed for Paul (or rather as yet Saul), that that might be wrought in him which was wrought.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in that he says, “But I was unknown by face to the churches of Judæa which are in Christ: only they heard that he who persecuted us in times past, now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed; and they magnified God in me;” why did they magnify God, but because they asked this of God, before it came to pass?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our Lord then first of all cut off “much speaking,” that thou mightest not bring a multitude of words unto God, as though by thy many words thou wouldest teach Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore when thou prayest thou hast need of piety, not of wordiness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For your Father knoweth what is needful for you, before ye ask Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye loth then to use many words, for He knoweth what is needful for you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But lest peradventure any should say here, If He know what is needful for us, why should we use so much as a few words? why should we pray at all?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knoweth Himself; let Him then give what He knoweth to be needful for us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, but it is His will that thou shouldest pray, that He may give to thy longings, that His gifts may not be lightly esteemed; seeing He hath Himself formed this longing desire in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The words therefore which our Lord Jesus Christ hath taught us in His prayer, are the rule and standard of our desires.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou mayest not ask for anything but what is written there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do ye therefore say,” saith he, “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where ye see ye have begun to have God for your Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye will have Him, when ye are new born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Although even now before ye are born, ye have been conceived of His seed, as being on the eve of being brought forth in the font, the womb as it were of the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember then, that ye have a Father in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember that ye were born of your father Adam unto death, that ye are to be born anew of God the Father unto life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what ye say, say in your hearts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Only let there be the earnest affection of prayer, and there will be the effectual answer of Him who heareth prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hallowed be thy Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou ask, that God’s Name may be hallowed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then askest thou for that which is already holy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then when thou dost ask that His Name may be hallowed, dost thou not as it were pray to Him for Him, and not for thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No. Understand it aright, and it is for thine own self thou askest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this thou askest, that what is always in itself holy, may be hallowed in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “be hallowed?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Be accounted holy,” be not despised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then you see, that the good thou dost wish, thou wishest for thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou despise the Name of God, for thyself it will be ill, and not for God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom do we speak? and will not God’s kingdom come, if we ask it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of that kingdom do we speak which will be after the end of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God hath a kingdom always; neither is He ever without a kingdom, whom the whole creation serveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what kingdom then dost thou wish for?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That of which it is written in the Gospel, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom which is prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo here is the kingdom whereof we say, “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We pray that it may come in us; we pray that we may be found in it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For come it certainly will; but what will it profit thee, if it shall find thee at the left hand?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, here again it is for thine own self that thou wishest well; for thyself thou prayest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This it is that thou dost long for; this desire in thy prayer, that thou mayest so live, that thou mayest have a part in the kingdom of God, which is to be given to all saints.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore when thou dost say, “Thy kingdom come,” thou dost pray for thyself, that thou mayest live well.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us have part in Thy kingdom: let that come even to us, which is to come to Thy saints and righteous ones.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! if thou say not this, will not God do His will?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember what thou hast repeated in the Creed, “I believe in God the Father Almighty.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He be Almighty, why prayest thou that His will may be done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this then, “Thy will be done”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May it be done in me, that I may not resist Thy will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore here again it is for thyself thou prayest, and not for God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the will of God will be done in thee, though it be not done by thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For both in them to whom He shall say, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom prepared for you from the beginning of the world;” shall the will of God be done, that the saints and righteous may receive the kingdom; and in them to whom He shall say, “Depart ye into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels,” shall the will of God be done, that the wicked may be condemned to everlasting fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That His will may be done by thee is another thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not then without a cause, but that it may be well with thee, that thou dost pray that His will may be done in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whether it be well or ill with thee, it will still be done in thee: but O that it may be done by thee also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why do I say then, “Thy will be done in heaven and in earth,” and do not say, “Thy will be done by heaven and earth?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because what is done by thee, He Himself doeth in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Never is anything done by thee which He Himself doeth not in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sometimes, indeed, He doeth in thee what is not done by thee; but never is anything done by thee, if He do it not in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what is “in heaven and in earth,” or, “as in heaven so in earth?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Angels do Thy will; may we do it also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The mind is heaven, the flesh is earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou dost say (if so be thou do say it) with the Apostle, “With my mind I serve the law of God, but with the flesh the law of sin;” the will of God is done in heaven, but not yet in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the flesh shall be in harmony with the mind, and “death shall be swallowed up in victory,” so that no carnal desires shall remain for the mind to be in conflict with, when strife in the earth shall have passed away, the war of the heart be over, and that be gone by which is spoken, “the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; for these are contrary the one to the other; so that ye cannot do the things that ye would;” when this war, I say, shall be over, and all concupiscence shall have been changed into charity, nothing shall remain in the body to oppose the spirit, nothing to be tamed, nothing to be bridled, nothing to be trodden down; but the whole shall go on through concord unto righteousness, and the will of God will be done in heaven and in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done in heaven and in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We wish for perfection, when we pray for this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the Church the spiritual are heaven, the carnal are earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth;” that as the spiritual do serve Thee, so the carnal being reformed may serve Thee also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is yet another very spiritual meaning of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are admonished to pray for our enemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Church is heaven, the enemies of the Church are earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May our enemies believe, as we also believe in Thee! may they become friends, and end their enmities!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are earth, therefore are they against us; may they become heaven, and they will be with us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give us this day our daily bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now here it is manifest, that it is for ourselves we pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou sayest, “Hallowed be Thy Name,” it requires explanation how it is that it is for thyself thou prayest, not for God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou sayest, “Thy will be done;” here again is there need of explanation, lest thou think that thou art wishing well to God in this prayer, that His will may be done, and not rather that thou art praying for thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou sayest, “Thy kingdom come;” this again must be explained, lest thou think that thou art wishing well to God in this prayer that He may reign.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But from this place and onwards to the end of the Prayer, it is plain that we are praying to God for our own selves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou sayest, “Give us this day our daily bread,” thou dost profess thyself to be God’s beggar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But be not ashamed at this; how rich soever any man be on earth, he is still God’s beggar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The beggar takes his stand before the rich man’s house; but the rich man himself stands before the door of the great rich One.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Petition is made to him, and he maketh his petition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he were not in need, he would not knock at the ears of God in prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what doth the rich man need?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am bold to say, the rich man needeth even daily bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how is it that he hath abundance of all things? whence but because God hath given it him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What should he have, if God withdrew His hand?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have not many laid down to sleep in wealth, and risen up in beggary?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that he doth not want, is due to God’s mercy, not to his own power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this bread, Dearly beloved, by which our body is filled, by which the flesh is recruited day by day; this bread, I say, God giveth not to those only who praise, but to those also who blaspheme Him; “Who maketh His sun to rise upon the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain upon the just and on the unjust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou praisest Him, and He feedeth thee; thou dost blaspheme Him, He feedeth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He waiteth for thee to repent; but if thou wilt not change thyself, He will condemn thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then both good and bad receive this bread from God, thinkest thou there is no other bread for which the children ask, of which the Lord said in the Gospel, “It is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it to dogs?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, surely there is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is that bread? and why is it called daily?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because this is necessary as the other; for without it we cannot live; without bread we cannot live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is shamelessness to ask for wealth from God; it is no shamelessness to ask for daily bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which ministereth to pride is one thing, that which ministereth to life another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, because this bread which may be seen and handled, is given both to the good and bad; there is a daily bread, for which the children pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is the word of God, which is dealt out to us day by day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our bread is daily bread; and by it live not our bodies, but our souls.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is necessary for us who are even now labourers in the vineyard,—it is our food, not our hire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he that hires the labourer into the vineyard owes him two things; food, that he faint not, and his hire, wherewith he may rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our daily food then in this earth is the word of God, which is dealt out always in the Churches: our hire after labour is called eternal life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, if by this our daily bread thou understand what the faithful receive, what ye shall receive, when ye have been baptized, it is with good reason that we ask and say, “Give us this day our daily bread;” that we may live in such sort, as that we be not separated from the Holy Altar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Touching this petition again we need no explanation, that it is for ourselves that we pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we beg that our debts may be forgiven us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For debtors are we, not in money, but in sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art saying perchance at this moment, And you too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We answer, Yes, we too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What, ye Holy Bishops, are ye debtors?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, we are debtors too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it far from thee, do not thyself this wrong.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do myself no wrong, but I say the truth; we are debtors: “If we say we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have been baptized, and yet are we debtors.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not that anything then remained, which was not remitted to us in Baptism, but because in our lives we are contracting ever what needs daily forgiveness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who are baptized, and forthwith depart out of this life, come up from the font without any debt; without any debt they leave the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who are baptized and are still kept in this life, contract defilements by reason of their mortal frailty, by which though the ship be not sunk, yet have they need of recourse to the pump.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For otherwise by little and little will that enter in by which the whole ship will be sunk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to offer this prayer, is to have recourse to the pump.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we ought not only to pray, but to do alms also, because when the pump is used to prevent the ship from sinking, both the voices and hands are at work.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now we are at work with our voices, when we say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And we are at work with our hands when we do this, “Break thy bread to the hungry, and bring the houseless poor into thine house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shut up alms in the heart of a poor man, and it shall intercede for thee unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Although therefore all our sins were forgiven in the “laver of regeneration,” we should be driven into great straits, if there were not given to us the daily cleansing of the Holy Prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Alms and prayers purge away sins; only let not such sins be committed, for which we must necessarily be separated from our daily Bread; avoid we all such debts to which a severe and certain condemnation is due.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Call not yourselves righteous, as though ye had no cause to say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though ye abstain from idolatry, from the consolations of astrologers, from the cures of enchanters, though ye abstain from the seductions of heretics, from the divisions of schismatics; though ye abstain from murders, from adulteries and fornications, from thefts and plunderings, from false witnessings, and all such other sins which I do not name, as have a ruinous consequence, for which it is necessary that the sinner be cut off from the altar, and be so bound in earth, as to be bound in heaven, to his great and deadly danger, unless again he be so loosed in earth, as to be loosed in heaven; yet after all these are excepted, still there is no want of occasions whereby a man may sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man sins in seeing with pleasure what he ought not to see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet who can hold in the quickness of the eye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For from this the eye is said to have received its very name, from its quickness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can restrain the ear or eye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The eyes may be shut when thou wilt, and are shut in a moment, but the ears thou canst only with an effort close: thou must raise the hand and reach them, and if any one hold thy hand, they are kept open, nor canst thou close them against reviling, impure, or flattering, and seducing words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when thou hearest any things thou oughtest not to hear, though thou do it not, dost thou not sin with the ear? for thou hearest something that is bad with pleasure?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How great sins doth the deadly tongue commit!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, sometimes sins of such a nature, that a man is separated from the altar for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the tongue pertains the whole matter of blasphemies, and many idle words again are spoken, which are not convenient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let the hand do nothing wrong, let the feet run not to any evil, nor the eye be directed to immodesty; let not the ear be open with pleasure to filthy talk; nor the tongue move to indecent speech; yet tell me, who can restrain the thoughts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How often do we pray, my brethren, and our thoughts are elsewhere, as though we forgot before whom we are standing, or before whom we are prostrating ourselves!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If all these things be collected together against us, will they not therefore not overwhelm us, because they are small faults?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What matter is it whether lead or sand overwhelm us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lead is all one mass, the sand is small grains, but by their great number they overwhelm thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So thy sins are small.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seest thou not how the rivers are filled, and the lands are wasted by small drops?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are small, but they are many.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us therefore say every day; and say it in sincerity of heart, and do what we say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is an engagement, a covenant, an agreement that we make with God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord thy God saith to thee, Forgive, and I will forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast not forgiven; thou retainest thy sins against thyself, not I.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I pray thee, my dearly beloved children, since I know what is expedient for you in the Lord’s Prayer, and most of all in that sentence of it, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors;” hear me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are about to be baptized, forgive everything; whatsoever any man have in his heart against any other, let him from his heart forgive it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So enter in, and be sure, that all your sins which ye have contracted, whether from your birth of your parents after Adam with original sin, for which sins’ sake ye run with babes to the Saviour’s grace, or whatever after sins ye have contracted in your lives, by word, or deed, or thought, all are forgiven; and you will go out of the water as from before the presence of your Lord, with the sure discharge of all debts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now because by reason of those daily sins of which I have spoken, it is necessary for you to say, in that daily prayer of cleansing as it were, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors;” what will ye do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have enemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who can live on this earth without them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take heed to yourselves, love them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In no way can thine enemy so hurt thee by his violence, as thou dost hurt thyself if thou love him not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he may injure thy estate, or flocks, or house, or thy man-servant, or thy maid-servant, or thy son, or thy wife; or at most, if such power be given him, thy body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But can he injure thy soul, as thou canst thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Reach forward, dearly beloved, I beseech you, to this perfection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But have I given you this power?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He only hath given it to whom ye say, “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet let it not seem impossible to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know, I have known by experience, that there are Christian men who do love their enemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it seem to you impossible, ye will not do it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe then first that it can be done, and pray that the will of God may be done in you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what good can thy neighbour’s ill do thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he had no ill, he would not even be thine enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wish him well then, that he may end his ill, and he will be thine enemy no longer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not the human nature in him that is at enmity with thee, but his sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is he therefore thine enemy, because he hath a soul and body?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this he is as thou art: thou hast a soul, and so hath he: thou hast a body, and so hath he.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is of the same substance as thou art; ye were made both out of the same earth, and quickened by the same Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In all this he is as thou art.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge in him then thy brother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first pair, Adam and Eve, were our parents; the one our father, the other our mother; and therefore we are brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let us leave the consideration of our first origin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God is our Father, the Church our Mother, and therefore are we brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But you will say, my enemy is a heathen, a Jew, a heretic, of whom I spake some time ago on the words, “Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Church, thy enemy is the heathen, the Jew, the heretic; he is the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art heaven, call on thy Father which is in heaven, and pray for thine enemies: for so was Saul an enemy of the Church; thus was prayer made for him, and he became her friend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He not only ceased from being her persecutor, but he laboured to be her helper.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet, to say the truth, prayer was made against him; but against his malice, not against his nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So let thy prayer be against the malice of thine enemy, that it may die, and he may live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thine enemy were dead, thou hast lost it might seem an enemy, yet hast thou not found a friend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if his malice die, thou hast at once lost an enemy and found a friend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But still ye are saying, Who can do, who has ever done this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May God bring it to effect in your hearts!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know as well as you, there are but few who do it; great men are they and spiritual who do so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are all the faithful in the Church who approach the altar, and take the Body and Blood of Christ, are they all such?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet they all say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What, if God should answer them, “Why do ye ask me to do what I have promised, when ye do not what I have commanded?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What have I promised?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “To forgive your debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What have I commanded?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That ye also forgive your debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can ye do this, if ye do not love your enemies?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then must we do, brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is the flock of Christ reduced to such a scanty number?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they only ought to say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors,” who love their enemies; I know not what to do, I know not what to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For must I say to you, If ye do not love your enemies, do not pray; I dare not say so; yea, pray rather that ye may love them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But must I say to you, If ye do not love your enemies, say not in the Lord’s Prayer, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose that I were to say, Do not use these words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye do not, your debts are not forgiven; and if ye do use them, and do not act thereafter, they are not forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In order therefore that they may be forgiven, ye must both use the prayer, and do thereafter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see some ground on which I may comfort not some few only, but the multitude of Christians: and I know that ye are longing to hear it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ hath said, “Forgive, that ye may be forgiven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what do ye say in the Prayer which we have now been discussing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So, Lord, forgive, as we forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This thou sayest, “O Father, which art in heaven, so forgive our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this ye ought to do, and if ye do it not, ye will perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When your enemy asks pardon, at once forgive him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And is this much for you to do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though it were much for thee to love thine enemy when violent against thee, is it much to love a man who is a supplicant before thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hast thou to say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was before violent, and then thou hatedst him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I had rather thou hadst not hated him even then: I had rather then when thou wert suffering from his violence, thou hadst remembered the Lord, saying, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would have then much wished that even at that time when thine enemy was violent against thee, thou hadst had regard to the Lord thy God speaking thus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps you will say, He did it, but then He did it as being the Lord, as the Christ, as the Son of God, as the Only-Begotten, as the Word made flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what can I, an infirm and sinful man, do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thy Lord be too high an example for thee, turn thy thoughts upon thy fellow-servant.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The holy Stephen was being stoned, and as they stoned him, on bended knees did he pray for his enemies, and say, “Lord, lay not this sin to their charge.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were casting stones, not asking pardon, yet did he pray for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would thou wert like him; reach forth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou for ever trailing thy heart along the earth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear, “Lift up thy heart,” reach forward, love thine enemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou canst not love him in his violence, love him at least when he asks pardon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Love the man who saith to thee, “Brother, I have sinned, forgive me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou then forgive him not, I say not merely, that thou dost blot this prayer out of thine heart, but thou shall be blotted thyself out of the book of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou then at least forgive him, or let go hatred from thy heart, it is hatred from the heart I bid thee forego, and not proper discipline.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What if one who asks my pardon, be one who ought to be chastised by me!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do what thou wilt, for I suppose that thou dost love thy child even when thou dost chastise him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou regardest not his cries under the rod, because thou art reserving for him his inheritance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This I say then, that thou forego from thy heart all hatred, when thine enemy asks pardon of thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps you will say, “he is playing false, he is pretending.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O thou judge of another’s heart, tell me thine own father’s thoughts, tell me thine own thoughts yesterday.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He asks and petitions for pardon; forgive, by all means forgive him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wilt not forgive him, it is thyself thou dost hurt, not him, for he knows what he has to do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art not willing to forgive thine own fellow-servant; he will go then to thy Lord, and say to Him, “Lord, I have prayed my fellow-servant to forgive me, and he would not; do Thou forgive me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hath not the Lord power to release his servant’s debts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So he, having obtained pardon from his Lord, returns loosed, whilst thou remainest bound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The time of prayer will come, the time must come for thee to say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors;” and the Lord will answer thee, Thou wicked servant, when thou didst owe Me so great a debt, thou didst ask Me, and I forgave thee; “shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellow-servant, even as I had pity on thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These words are out of the Gospel, not of my own heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if on being asked, thou shall forgive him who begs for pardon, then thou canst say this prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if thou hast not as yet the strength to love him in his violence, still thou mayest offer this prayer, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us pass on to the rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And lead us not into temptation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors,” we say because of past sins, which we cannot undo, that they should not have been done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst labour not to do what thou hast done before, but how canst thou bring about, that that which thou hast done should not be done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As regards those things which have been done already, that sentence of the prayer is thy help, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As regards those into which thou mayest fall, what wilt thou do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil,” that is, from temptation itself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now these three first petitions, “Hallowed be Thy Name, Thy kingdom come, Thy will be done as in heaven so in earth,” these three regard the life eternal, for God’s Name ought to be hallowed in us always, we ought to be in His kingdom always, we ought to do His will always.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will be to all eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “daily bread” is necessary now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the rest that we pray for from this article, regards the necessities of the present life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Daily bread is necessary in this life; the forgiveness of our debts is necessary in this life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when we shall arrive at the other life, there will be an end of all debts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this life there is temptation, in this life the sailing is dangerous, in this life something is ever stealing its way in through the chinks of our frailties, which must be pumped out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when we shall be made equal to the Angels of God; no more need to say and pray to God to forgive us our debts, when there will be none.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here then is the “daily bread;” here the prayer that our “debts may be forgiven;” here that we “enter not into temptation;” for in that life temptation does not enter; here that we may be “delivered from evil;” for in that life there will be no evil, but eternal and abiding good.
